---
title: "Research profile"
site: distill::distill_website
---

<style>
body {
  text-align: justify}

h1 {
  text-align: center
}

.aligncenter {
    text-align: center;
}
</style>

<p class="aligncenter"><img src="images/profile.png" alt="Profile picture" align="middle" style="width:145px;height:190px;border:0.7px solid black;"></p>

I'm a post-doctoral researcher working in the [Making Numbers Meaningful](https://bodowinter.com/FLF.html) project led by [Bodo Winter](https://bodowinter.com/) at the [Department of English Language and Linguistics](https://www.birmingham.ac.uk/schools/edacs/departments/englishlanguage/index.aspx), University of Birmingham. 

I'm broadly interested in how people create and manage meaning through language, both when using language to communicate with others in interaction and when producing and interpreting language in other settings. In my research I use primarily quantitative empirical methods, including controlled experimentation and corpus analysis, to investigate:

* how linguistic and extralinguistic contextual cues modulate people's discourse expectations and in turn affect their understanding and their ascription of actions and mental states;

* how pragmatic and interactional aspects of human communication affect the emergence and evolution of linguistic meaning and structure.

In my PhD, supervised by [Michael Franke](https://michael-franke.github.io/heimseite/) and [Manfred Krifka](https://www.leibniz-zas.de/en/people/details/krifka-manfred/manfred-krifka), I investigated pragmatic prediction in the incremental interpretation of utterances containing epistemicity markers in English and German.

I'm also interested in issues related to research design and methodology and data analysis, including teaching basic data analysis and statistical modeling.
